packages.name: @tahanabavi/typefetch
packages.summary: A strongly typed TypeScript HTTP client powered by Zod contracts, middleware, retries, mock data, response wrappers, field encryption, contract testing, CLI workflows, and report generation.

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: The package turns Zod endpoint contracts into a runtime-safe and type-safe API client.

blocks.text: TypeFetch is built around one idea: define every API endpoint once as a Zod contract, then let the client generate typed module methods, validate request input, validate successful responses, run middleware, and power contract-driven tests from the same source of truth.

items: Typed modules are generated from the contract object after client.init().
Requests are validated before fetch is called.
Responses are validated before data reaches application code.
Structured input supports path, query, body, and headers.
The same endpoint metadata powers mock mode, encryption, CLI tests, and reports.

sections.title: Installation
sections.description: Install TypeFetch and Zod. Zod is a peer dependency.

blocks.code: npm install @tahanabavi/typefetch zod
yarn add @tahanabavi/typefetch zod
pnpm add @tahanabavi/typefetch zod

sections.title: Exports
sections.description: The public API is exported from the root package, with a dedicated middleware subpath.

rows: Main client class that builds typed modules from contracts.
Normalized error class used for HTTP, validation, wrapper, token, and network failures.
Helper for creating typed structured request schemas.
Logs outgoing requests and incoming responses.
Injects an Authorization bearer token from a refresh function.
Caches successful GET responses in memory.
Encrypts selected request fields and decrypts selected response fields.
Contract-driven API test runner and report types.
Helper for CLI test configuration files.

blocks.code:
import { ApiClient, RichError, makeRequestSchema } from "@tahanabavi/typefetch";
import { cacheMiddleware, encryptionMiddleware } from "@tahanabavi/typefetch/middlewares";

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.description: Create contracts, create the client, initialize modules, then call generated methods.

blocks.title: Important
blocks.text: Always call client.init() before reading client.modules. init() creates the module and endpoint methods from your contract object.

sections.title: Contract Shape
sections.description: A contract is a module map. Each module contains endpoint definitions.

rows: HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE.
Endpoint path. Params use colon syntax like /users/:id.
Zod schema for the endpoint input.
Zod schema for the successful response payload.
When true, the client requires a token and sends Authorization: Bearer <token>.
Static response data or a function returning response data for mock mode.
Static headers or a function derived from parsed input.
json by default, or form-data for multipart payloads.
Contract-driven test metadata for the test runner and CLI.

sections.title: Structured Requests
sections.description: Structured input separates URL params, query params, JSON/form body, and per-call headers.

rows: Replaces :param placeholders in endpoint.path. Missing values throw MISSING_PATH_PARAM.
Appends URLSearchParams. Arrays repeat the key. Dates become ISO strings. Objects become JSON strings.
Sent as JSON for non-GET requests unless bodyType is form-data.
Merged into request headers after endpoint headers.
Input is treated as structured only when its keys are limited to path, query, body, headers, or header.

sections.title: Request Schema Helper
sections.description: makeRequestSchema provides a typed helper for structured request schemas.

blocks.text: The helper creates a Zod object with optional path and query objects, a typed body, and optional record headers. If no body is provided, body becomes optional undefined.

sections.title: Flat Request Compatibility
sections.description: Non-structured schemas still work for older APIs or simple endpoints.

blocks.text: If input does not look like a structured request, TypeFetch treats the full parsed input as the payload. For non-GET methods it is JSON stringified as the body. For GET methods it is validated but not sent as a body.

sections.title: ApiClient API
sections.description: The client exposes runtime controls for modules, middleware, errors, transforms, retries, tokens, mocks, and wrappers.

rows: Builds client.modules from the contract tree.
Typed generated endpoint methods grouped by module.
Receives normalized errors before they are thrown.
Transforms parsed successful response data before returning it.
Enables built-in retry around the whole request execution.
Sets or replaces the dynamic token provider.
Toggles mock mode and optional mock latency.
Defines a wrapper schema used to validate and unwrap API envelopes.
Returns tokenProvider result first, otherwise config.token.

sections.title: Client Configuration
sections.description: Constructor config defines base URL, auth token source, and mock defaults.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.description: Endpoint auth and auth middleware are separate tools with different jobs.

blocks.text: When endpoint.auth is true, ApiClient requires a token from tokenProvider or config.token. If no token exists, it throws RichError with status 401 and code NO_TOKEN before fetch runs.
authMiddleware can also inject Authorization from a refreshToken function. If refreshToken fails, the middleware logs a warning and lets the request continue.

sections.title: Headers
sections.description: Headers can come from defaults, endpoint config, structured request input, auth, and middleware.

items: Content-Type: application/json is added unless bodyType is form-data.
Endpoint headers can be a fixed record or a function of parsed input.
Structured input headers are normalized and merged after endpoint headers.
auth: true adds Authorization after token resolution.
Middleware can still mutate ctx.init.headers before fetch.

sections.title: Body Serialization
sections.description: TypeFetch serializes JSON and form-data bodies based on endpoint.bodyType.

rows: parts.body or flat input is JSON.stringify'd.
Used directly when bodyType is form-data.

sections.title: Middleware Pipeline
sections.description: Middlewares wrap fetch in registration order, then unwind in reverse order.

blocks.text: The client builds the runner with reduceRight. If you register firstMiddleware then secondMiddleware, the request enters first, then second, then fetch; responses unwind through second then first.

rows: Final URL after path and query handling.
Mutable RequestInit with method, headers, body, and signal.
The endpoint definition, including metadata such as encryption/test.

sections.title: Built-in Retry Engine
sections.description: ApiClient has a retry engine independent of retryMiddleware.

rows: Number of retry attempts after the first failure.
Optional function receiving RichError and attempt.

sections.title: Timeout and Abort
sections.description: Each generated endpoint method accepts optional request options.

blocks.text: When timeout is provided, the client creates an AbortController and clears its timer after success or failure. If both signal and timeout exist, the passed signal takes priority in ctx.init.signal.

sections.title: Mock Mode
sections.description: Mock mode returns endpoint mockData without performing network requests.

blocks.text: Mock data can be static or a function. It is delayed using the configured mockDelay range, parsed with the endpoint response schema, then passed through responseTransform.

sections.title: Response Wrappers
sections.description: Validate API envelopes and return only the successful data payload.

blocks.text: setResponseWrapper receives the endpoint success schema and returns a Zod schema for the whole API envelope. If the parsed wrapper has success: false, TypeFetch throws RichError. If success is true, it validates and returns parsedWrapped.data.

sections.title: Errors
sections.description: Errors are normalized into RichError whenever possible.

rows: status, code, title, detail, errors from JSON response
message from thrown error or Unknown error

sections.title: Logging Middleware
sections.description: loggingMiddleware logs request and response information when debug is enabled.

sections.title: Cache Middleware
sections.description: cacheMiddleware stores successful GET JSON responses in memory by method and URL.

items: Only GET requests are cached.
Cache key is METHOD:URL.
Default TTL is 60000ms.
Cached hits return a synthetic Response with JSON content.
Each middleware instance owns its own in-memory cache.

blocks.code: client.use(cacheMiddleware({ ttl: 120_000 }));

sections.title: Retry Middleware
sections.description: retryMiddleware retries middleware/fetch failures with exponential delay based on a fixed base delay.

sections.title: Encryption Middleware
sections.description: Encrypt selected request body fields and decrypt selected response fields.

rows: symmetric key or rsa public/private pair
Parses JSON body, applies request map, then writes encrypted JSON back to ctx.init.body.
Clones JSON response, applies response map, and returns a new Response.
Defaults to true. Throws instead of leaking plaintext on failure.

sections.title: Encryption Maps
sections.description: Maps describe which fields should be transformed and which method should be used.

rows: Transform this field using an explicit method.
Apply item map by index or reuse first item map.

sections.title: Endpoint Test Metadata
sections.description: Endpoint test config lets contracts define how API tests should run.

rows: Disable all tests for the endpoint when false.
Filter endpoints/cases with includeTags and excludeTags.
Marks unsafe endpoints such as DELETE/reset/payment.
Default static input or async factory using shared context.
Lifecycle hooks that can read/write shared context.

sections.title: Test Runner
sections.description: The runner supports schema, mock, live, and full modes.

rows: Validates generated or configured input against request schema without network calls.
Validates endpoint mockData against response schema.
Calls client.modules endpoint methods using generated/configured input.
Runs schema, mock, and live phases when applicable.
Default request timeout for live calls.
Controls generated input values from Zod schemas.

sections.title: Auto Input Generation
sections.description: The tester can generate valid inputs from Zod schemas.

rows: Whether optional object fields should be generated. Default true.
Whether arrays receive one generated item. Default true.
Override by full dot path, field name, or request part path.

sections.title: CLI
sections.description: The typefetch binary runs init, list, test, release-doc, help, and version commands.

blocks.code:
typefetch init
typefetch list
typefetch test --mode full --format markdown,json,html --output ./typefetch-report/report

sections.title: CLI Flags
sections.description: The parser supports long flags, aliases, boolean flags, repeated flags, and --no-* negation.

rows: Report format. Repeated values become an array.
Sets a boolean flag to false.

sections.title: CLI Config
sections.description: A config file can provide contracts, client creation, runner options, context, and report defaults.

sections.title: Reports
sections.description: API tests can produce JSON, Markdown, and HTML reports.

rows: Per-case result with module, endpoint, phase, status, input, response, or error.

blocks.code: typefetch test --mode full --format markdown,json,html --output ./typefetch-report/report

sections.title: Type Inference
sections.description: Endpoint method input and output types are inferred from Zod schemas.

blocks.text: EndpointMethods maps each endpoint to a function that accepts z.infer<request> and returns Promise<z.infer<response>>. That means editor autocomplete follows your schemas for both request input and response output.

sections.title: Unit Testing Clients
sections.description: Because TypeFetch uses fetch, tests can mock fetch and still exercise validation and parsing.

items: Request validation
Response validation
Path parameter handling
Query string generation
JSON and FormData serialization
Header merging
Auth token injection
Middleware execution order
Retries, timeout, and abort behavior
Mock mode, wrappers, errors, and encryption

sections.title: Best Practices
sections.description: Recommended usage patterns for reliable TypeFetch projects.

items: Prefer structured request schemas for new endpoints.
Keep contracts in a shared module and export a single initialized api object.
Use endpoint test metadata for critical flows and CLI smoke checks.
Mark destructive endpoints so they do not run by default.
Use response wrappers when your backend returns API envelopes.
Keep encryption failClosed true outside debugging sessions.
Avoid caching sensitive GET responses with cacheMiddleware.
Use createClient in CLI config when baseUrl/token should come from CLI flags.

sections.title: v1.6.0 Overview
sections.description: Version 1.6.0 introduced the contract-driven testing layer and CLI workflows.

items: Endpoint discovery from contracts.
Generated and custom request input.
Schema, mock, live, and full test modes.
Shared test context for multi-step flows.
Markdown, HTML, and JSON reports.
Versioned release documentation under docs/releases.

sections.title: Migration Notes
sections.description: Existing clients continue to work while projects can opt into testing metadata.

blocks.text: The main client API remains compatible with existing contract usage. New fields such as endpoint.test and CLI config are additive, so teams can introduce contract tests gradually.

packages.name: @tahanabavi/typefetch
packages.summary: Ein stark typisierter TypeScript HTTP Client auf Basis von Zod Contracts, Middleware, Retries, Mock-Daten, Response Wrappern, Field Encryption, Contract Testing, CLI Workflows und Reports.

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: TypeFetch verwandelt Zod Endpoint Contracts in einen runtime-sicheren und typisierten API Client.

blocks.text: Die Dokumentation ist jetzt komplett JSON-basiert und kann pro Sprache separat gepflegt werden. Die englische Version enthält die vollständige technische Referenz; diese deutsche Struktur ist bereit für vollständige Übersetzung.

items: Contracts erzeugen typisierte Module.
Requests und Responses werden mit Zod validiert.
Middleware, Mock Mode, Encryption, CLI und Tests nutzen dieselbe Endpoint-Metadatenstruktur.

sections.title: v1.6.0 Overview
sections.description: Version 1.6.0 führte Contract Testing und CLI Workflows ein.

blocks.text: Diese Version kann aus Contracts Endpoints erkennen, Inputs erzeugen und Reports in mehreren Formaten exportieren.
